evals.name: django-api-endpoint-review
evals.prompt:
Can you review this Django view I'm adding? It's a new API endpoint for our project management app. We have about 500 users and growing. Here's the code:

```python
from django.http import JsonResponse
from django.views import View
import json

class TaskListView(View):
    def get(self, request):
        project_id = request.GET.get('project_id')
        tasks = Task.objects.filter(project_id=project_id)
        result = []
        for task in tasks:
            assignee = User.objects.get(id=task.assignee_id)
            comments = Comment.objects.filter(task_id=task.id)
            result.append({
                'id': task.id,
                'title': task.title,
                'assignee_name': assignee.first_name + ' ' + assignee.last_name,
                'comment_count': comments.count(),
                'project': task.project.name,
                'created': str(task.created_at),
            })
        return JsonResponse({'tasks': result})

    def post(self, request):
        data = json.loads(request.body)
        task = Task.objects.create(
            title=data['title'],
            description=data['description'],
            project_id=data['project_id'],
            assignee_id=data['assignee_id'],
            status=data.get('status', 'todo'),
            priority=data.get('priority', 'medium'),
            is_admin=data.get('is_admin', False),
        )
        return JsonResponse({'id': task.id, 'title': task.title}, status=201)
```
evals.expected_output: Should identify multiple issues across review dimensions: (1) Performance: N+1 queries in the GET — User.objects.get and Comment.objects.filter inside the loop, plus task.project.name triggering another query. Recommend select_related/prefetch_related and annotate. (2) Security: No authentication check on either method, mass assignment vulnerability on POST (is_admin field from user input), no input validation. (3) Code quality: Missing pagination for growing dataset, no error handling for missing task/user. Should prioritize findings by severity and provide concrete fix suggestions with code examples.

assertions.text: Identifies N+1 query problem in the GET method (queries inside the for loop)
Recommends select_related or prefetch_related as the fix for N+1
Flags the mass assignment vulnerability (is_admin field from user input in POST)
Notes missing authentication/authorization on the endpoints
Points out missing pagination for the task list
Categorizes findings by severity (must-fix vs should-fix vs suggestion)
Provides concrete code examples or fix suggestions, not just problem descriptions

evals.name: react-component-performance-review
evals.expected_output: Should identify multiple performance issues: (1) Giant context (AppContext with user, theme, notifications, cart, settings) — any context change re-renders this component. (2) filter+sort on every render without memoization. (3) Inline style object and inline arrow function in map creating new references every render. (4) No virtualization for 2000 items — rendering all of them to DOM. (5) No debounce on search input. Should suggest useMemo for filtered list, React.memo for ProductCard, virtualizer (react-window/tanstack-virtual), context splitting, and search debouncing. Should note that React Compiler (React 19) may handle some memoization automatically.

assertions.text: Identifies the oversized context (AppContext) as a re-render source
Flags the filter+sort computation running on every render without memoization
Points out inline objects/functions in JSX (style prop, onAddToCart) causing re-renders
Recommends virtualization for the large product list (react-window, tanstack-virtual, or similar)
Suggests debouncing the search input
Acknowledges what the component does well or provides constructive tone (not just a list of problems)

evals.name: express-auth-security-review
evals.expected_output: Should flag this as critical-severity with multiple urgent security issues: (1) CRITICAL: SQL injection in both queries (string interpolation in SQL). (2) CRITICAL: Plaintext password storage and comparison. (3) CRITICAL: Hardcoded JWT secret and database credentials in source code. (4) MAJOR: No authorization check (any authenticated user can access/modify any other user — IDOR). (5) MAJOR: SELECT * returning password hash and all sensitive fields. (6) MAJOR: Error details exposed (err.message in auth error). (7) SHOULD-FIX: No rate limiting on password change, no password complexity validation, single database connection (no pooling). Should recommend blocking this PR from merge and provide specific fixes for each issue.

assertions.text: Identifies SQL injection vulnerability (string interpolation in SQL queries)
Flags plaintext password storage and comparison as critical
Identifies hardcoded secrets (JWT_SECRET, database credentials) in source code
Points out missing authorization check (IDOR — any user can access any other user's data)
Recommends blocking merge / treating as critical severity
Provides concrete fix suggestions (parameterized queries, bcrypt, env vars) not just problem descriptions
Identifies SELECT * as exposing sensitive data unnecessarily
